title: Ensure That Microsoft Defender for Resource Manager Is  Set To 'On'

input_row.title: Ensure That Microsoft Defender for Resource Manager Is  Set To 'On'
input_row.description: Microsoft Defender for Resource Manager scans incoming administrative requests to change your infrastructure from both CLI and the Azure portal.
input_row.rationale: Scanning resource requests lets you be alerted every time there is suspicious activity in order to prevent a security threat from being introduced.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. Go to Microsoft Defender for Cloud. 2. Under Management, select Environment Settings. 3. Click on the subscription name. 4. Select the Defender plans blade. 5. Ensure Status is set to On for Resource Manager. Audit from Azure CLI Ensure the output of the below command is Standard az security pricing show -n 'Arm' --query 'pricingTier' Audit from PowerShell Get-AzSecurityPricing -Name 'Arm' | Select-Object Name,PricingTier Ensure the output of PricingTier is Standard Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: c3d20c29-b36d-48fe-808b-99a87530ad99 - Name: 'Azure Defender for Resource Manager should be enabled'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. Go to Microsoft Defender for Cloud. 2. Under Management, select Environment Settings. 3. Click on the subscription name. 4. Select the Defender plans blade. 5. Select On under Status for Resource Manager. 6. Select Save. Remediate from Azure CLI Use the below command to enable Standard pricing tier for Defender for Resource Manager az security pricing create -n 'Arm' --tier 'Standard' Remediate from PowerShell Use the below command to enable Standard pricing tier for Defender for Resource Manager Set-AzSecurityPricing -Name 'Arm' -PricingTier 'Standard'
input_row.impact: Enabling Microsoft Defender for Resource Manager requires enabling Microsoft Defender for your subscription. Both will incur additional charges.
input_row.default_value: By default, Microsoft Defender for Resource Manager is not enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_security_defender_resource_manager_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API Calls/Boto3 SDK Methods to Use:**
  - Utilize the AWS Config service to check the configuration of AWS resources and ensure that the equivalent defense mechanisms, such as AWS Security Hub and its integration with AWS Config for monitoring security of resources, are enabled.
  - Utilize `boto3` library methods like `client.describe_region()` to programmatically check if AWS Security Hub is activated in the region, since AWS lacks a direct equivalent to Microsoft Defender for Resource Manager but has similar services.

- **AWS CLI Commands (if applicable):**
  - Check if AWS Security Hub is enabled by running the following command:
    ```bash
    aws securityhub get-enabled-standards
    ```

- **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
  - Security Hub status.
  
- **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
  - Confirm that AWS Security Hub or a similar service is enabled and actively monitoring for changes across your AWS environment.

- **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
  - **Pass Criteria:** The response from `aws securityhub get-enabled-standards` should confirm that the relevant standards for resource monitoring (e.g., CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark) are enabled.
  - **Fail Criteria:** Any response indicating that the standards are not active or AWS Security Hub is not enabled.

- **Example:**
  - Verify that necessary security standards in AWS Security Hub are active, indicating that the equivalent defense mechanisms are in place for monitoring suspicious activity similar to Microsoft Defender for Resource Manager. Check responses that specify enabled standards and their configurations.
